summary: Critical financial anomalies detected including severe balance shortfall for COE requirements and evidence of structured deposits (smurfing), alongside informational gaps for missing enrollment documents.

anomalies.explanation: Document not provided — required for complete audit.
Closing balance of 141,083.88 INR is severely below the estimated equivalent of the ¥2,000,000 (approx. 1,100,000 INR) minimum requirement for a Japanese Student COE.
Five identical incoming transfers of 30,000 INR on 03-03-2026 indicate smurfing or structured deposits designed to obscure the source of funds.
198 unlabeled deposits are present, including successive 50,000 INR deposits in October 2025, violating the requirement that all large deposits have documented sources.
Document not provided — required for complete audit.

full_finding.summary: Critical financial anomalies detected including severe balance shortfall for COE requirements and evidence of structured deposits (smurfing), alongside informational gaps for missing enrollment documents.
